home.title: A suite of tools to quantify your investment decisions
home.subtitle: This project brings the S'investir simulators into a unified, dark and embeddable interface. Pick a destination below — each page has a specific role.
home.footerNote: The crypto simulator is embeddable via iframe — open it, then click “Embed this simulator”.

presentation.title: The project explained end to end
presentation.description: An interactive canvas presents the whole project the way a product manager would: the brand, the design system, the pages and the discovery journey, with live previews of every screen.

architecture.title: How the project is built
architecture.description: The tech lead view: folder structure, token-driven design system and a decoupled data layer — so you can swap the API without breaking anything.

crypto.title: Crypto DCA simulator
crypto.description: Simulate a recurring investment (Dollar Cost Averaging) on a crypto asset using real market data.
points: Configurable asset, amount, frequency and period
Key figures: capital, quantity, average price, gains/losses
History & gains/losses charts with time zoom

decouverte.title: Discovery journey
decouverte.description: A guided version of the crypto simulator: we onboard a user discovering DCA step by step, without jargon.
points: Three steps: the stake, the crypto, the result
Top 5 cryptos to choose in one click
Each step lives in the URL — replayable and embeddable

sinvestir.title: S'investir landing page
sinvestir.description: The showcase page of the suite: positioning, presentation of the tools and calls to action.
points: Serves as the design reference for the other pages

designsystem.title: Design System
designsystem.description: The living style guide: colors, typography, components and tokens shared across the whole project.
points: Live preview — editing a token updates everything

crypto.title: Simulate a recurring crypto investment
crypto.subtitle: Choose an asset, an amount and a frequency, then visualize the performance of a DCA strategy on real market data — quantity acquired, final capital, gains and losses.

amount.title: What if you had invested a little, regularly?
amount.description: This simulator goes back in time: it shows you what a small sum placed regularly in a crypto would have become today — without betting it all at once.
amount.amountLabel: How much could you have put in each time?

asset.title: Which crypto do you want to test?
asset.descriptionPrefix: Choose one of the best-known cryptos. We'll calculate what

results.title: Here's what it would have become
results.descriptionSuffix: since {{year}}, your portfolio would have followed these curves. Zoom in on a period to explore.

embed.title: Embed the simulator
embed.subtitle: Configure the display, copy the code, then paste it on your site.
embed.lockFieldsHint: — visitors see the result without being able to edit it

hero.title: The financial simulators and comparators to quantify your decisions
hero.subtitle: Simulate, save and share your investment calculations in a few clicks. Access clear, reliable and completely free tools!

community.title: A community of investors that grows every day
community.label: REGISTERED USERS

howItWorks.title: Your simulations, from calculation to sharing

steps.title: Simulate or compare
steps.desc: Choose a simulator or a comparator, and start your simulation.

steps.title: Save your simulation
steps.desc: Keep your calculations in your personal space to find them later.

steps.title: Share your results
steps.desc: Share a clear link of your assumptions and results in a few clicks.

toolbox.title: The S'investir community toolbox
toolbox.description: Clear simulators and detailed comparators, designed to make better decisions with a free account to centralize, save and share your simulations.

pea.title: PEA Comparator
pea.desc: Which broker to choose to open your PEA? This comparator analyzes the best offers on the market according to more than 40 criteria.

cto.title: CTO Comparator
cto.desc: Which broker to choose to open a securities account? This comparator analyzes offers according to fees, products, services and overall quality.

assuranceVie.title: Life Insurance Comparator
assuranceVie.desc: Which life insurance to choose? This comparator analyzes the best offers according to fees, euro funds, unit-linked accounts and service quality.

per.title: PER Comparator
per.desc: Which PER to choose? This comparator analyzes offers according to fees, euro funds, unit-linked accounts and overall quality.

scpi.title: SCPI Comparator
scpi.desc: Which SCPIs to choose? This comparator identifies the best-performing SCPIs to maximize your return.

crypto.title: Crypto Comparator
crypto.desc: Which crypto-asset platform to choose? This comparator analyzes offers according to accessibility, features, fees and security.

bank.title: Online Bank Comparator
bank.desc: Which is the best online bank and the one that suits you best?

proBank.title: Professional Online Bank Comparator
proBank.desc: Which professional online bank to choose for your business?

etf.title: Best ETF Comparator
etf.desc: Which ETFs to choose? This comparator gathers a selection of ETFs according to category, fees, assets, PEA eligibility and replication method.

items.a: Yes, all our simulators and comparators are accessible for free and without limit.
A free account lets you save your simulations, find them later and share them with a link.
No. Your simulations remain private; only you decide to share a result via a link.
The results are purely indicative and educational. They do not constitute investment advice.
Yes, new tools are added regularly based on the community's needs.

items.q: Are these simulators suitable for beginners?
items.a: Absolutely. Each tool is designed to be clear and accessible, whatever your level.

items.q: How long does it take to run a simulation?
items.a: A few minutes are enough: enter your assumptions and get an immediate result.

footer.disclaimer: The simulators offered are made available free of charge, for educational and informational purposes only. They aim to help users better understand certain concepts or estimate situations based on the information entered. They in no way constitute investment advice, tax advice or a personalized recommendation. Investing involves risks, including the risk of capital loss. Past performance is no guarantee of future performance. The results obtained are purely indicative and must be analyzed with discernment.
